title: The packaged catalog ships the convention-articulation trio at warning; error severity is a per-substrate raise, not a catalog default
context: The live substrate declares decision/feature/task-articulates-convention at error (the FEAT-007 backfill-then-raise end-state); the packaged catalog (samples/conception.json) declares the same trio at warning. update's registry propagation is additive-only and never overwrites a locally-diverged severity, so the divergence persists by construction and needs a recorded disposition: raise the catalog, or record the divergence as designed.
decision: The catalog keeps warning; the live/catalog divergence is the designed relationship, not drift. Derivation: on an empty substrate the trio at error refuses the very first decision/feature/task filing until a convention item or missing-convention gap exists to be cited — a forced filing ceremony before first real use, contradicting the catalog's offer-don't-impose ethos (accept-all is idempotent never-clobber; the advisory tier surfaces the advice without blocking writes). Error severity is a per-substrate maturity step taken when the substrate's existing items are compliant: backfill-then-raise (the TASK-041 pattern; FEAT-007's end-state on this substrate). Any raise — catalog or local — additionally requires the satisfying transition atom per error-invariant-transition-atom.
consequences: New substrates adopt the trio as advisory and raise locally when mature; update's additive-only registry merge preserves the local raise on every subsequent update.
Catalog invariant severities are authored per this rule: advisory in the catalog; error by substrate-local backfill-then-raise with the transition atom in place.
No code or catalog change; the divergence needs no reconciliation and is not re-opened by future substrate-currency audits.
